briefdescription.para: Sets the orientation of the stack layout.
Gets the orientation of the stack layout.
Sets the spacing between children in the stack.
Gets the spacing between children in the stack.
Creates a new StackLayout with the specified orientation.

parameterdescription.para: The orientation of the stack (default: Vertical)

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated StackLayout

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to StackLayout handle.

simplesect.para: A handle to a StackLayout or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: StackLayout arranges its children in a single row or column.

detaileddescription.para: StackLayout is a simple layout container that stacks its children either horizontally or vertically with optional spacing between them.

sect1.title: Common Property Behavior
sect1.para: For a vertical StackLayout (horizontal is analogous with axes swapped):

listitem.para: RequestedHeight (main-axis): A positive value sets a fixed height. WRAP_CONTENT (-1) uses the child's natural size. MATCH_PARENT (-2) fills the parent.
RequestedWidth (cross-axis): A positive value sets a fixed width. WRAP_CONTENT (-1) uses the child's natural size. MATCH_PARENT (-2) fills the cross-axis.
StackLayoutParams alignment (cross-axis): START, CENTER, and END position the child within the cross-axis.
Margin: Applied on all sides, reducing the space available for the child.

Weight (via StackLayoutParams): Children with weight > 0 share the remaining main-axis space proportionally. The child's main-axis RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight is ignored — the size is determined entirely by the weight ratio. Only children with weight 0 are measured normally using their RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight.
